one_time_description: Cash settlement from Federal Government related to Indian Reserve No. 8 (I.R. 8) Claims Land under claim by Aitchelitz and four other First Nations
economic_notes: Band holds interests in forestry operations (Ch-ihl-Kway-Uhk Forestry LP), property management (Centre Creek LP, Ch-ihl-kway-uhk Tribe Society), environmental monitoring (Seven Generations Environmental Services Ltd.), and tribal management (Ts'elxweyeqx Tribe Limited Partnership). Settlement of $11,043,751 received from Federal Government for IR No. 8 land claim.
notes: Fiscal year is 2022 not 2024. Large one-time settlement of $11,043,751 from Federal Government for IR No. 8 land claim dominates revenue and surplus. Eight entities accounted for under modified equity method (GBEs). No schedule of remuneration provided. Total government transfers calculated as ISC ($454,078) + CMHC ($27,226) + Sto:lo Service Agency ($5,409) + Federal Government per segmented disclosure ($11,497,828 minus non-federal portions) — used segmented federal government revenue of $11,497,828 less ISC $454,078 less CMHC $27,226 less Sto:lo $5,409 already included, so total_government_transfers set to segmented federal total of $11,497,828 plus CMHC $27,226 adjusted; simplified as federal per segment $11,497,828 used. OSR = total revenue $12,677,257 minus total government transfers $11,524,132 = $1,153,125. Prior year revenue of $1,371,466 is unusually low compared to 2022 due to one-time settlement. Housing expenses combined: Housing community planning $479,173 + social housing $26,185 = $505,358.
warnings: One-time items ($1) represent 0.0% of revenue — surplus may not reflect ongoing operations
